data.title: RH gains after surprise Q1 profit, unchanged forecast
data.description:
** Shares of furniture retailer jump 18.2% to $209 premarket

** Co posts Q1 surprise profit of 13 cents/shr vs estimates of 9 cents/shr loss - data compiled by LSEG

** Q1 revenue of $814 mln slightly misses expectations of $818.1 mln

** Maintains FY25 forecast, but delays new concept launch to spring of FY26 from H2 FY25 due to tariff uncertainty

** Jefferies says shares higher due to unchanged guidance in face of challenging backdrop; plans to scale down capital expenditure encouraging

** Brokerage leaves rating and PT unchanged due to lack of catalysts

** Average rating of 20 analysts is "buy", median PT at $217.50 - data compiled by LSEG

** Stock has more than halved this year as of last close

data.title: Larger Middle East War Seen as Unlikely — Market Talk
data.description: A larger war in the Middle East seems unlikely because the U.S. administration has said it remains committed to talks with Iran, and Tehran's response to Israel's attack will likely be modest, Morningstar's Allen Good says. Oil markets remain amply supplied, with OPEC set on increasing production and demand soft, and prices aren't likely to rise much more. The global risk premium could rise, keeping prices moderately higher than where they have been much of the year, he adds. (cristina.gallardo@wsj.com)

data.title: Raymond James Initiates Entergy at Market Perform
data.description: Entergy has an average rating of overweight and mean price target of $92.35, according to analysts polled by FactSet.

data.title: Kim Jong Un Claims His Capsized Warship is Fixed — WSJ
data.description:
By Dasl Yoon

SEOUL — Strolling across a red carpet, Kim Jong Un boarded a 5,000-ton destroyer on Thursday and marveled at the breakneck speed of repairs on a North Korean warship that had been lying on its side, partially submerged last week.

Kim, wearing a black suit and a beige sun hat, claimed a quick victory over one of his biggest military embarrassments in years: the May 21 capsizing of a modern warship after a botched launch.

But the hulking destroyer — named "Kang Kon," after a revolutionary North Korean fighter — didn't leave the dry dock at the Rajin shipyard during Thursday's launch event. While the ship has been brought upright and repaired fairly quickly, its operational capability is still yet to be seen.

That didn't stop Kim, the 41-year-old dictator, from promising the Kang Kon vessel, as well as a second destroyer, would be delivered to North Korea's navy by next year, according to a report by Pyongyang's state media. He also vowed to start construction of two more warships.

"The new era of building a fleet," Kim was quoted as saying, "is making steady progress."

Kim desperately wants to modernize his Soviet-era naval forces, which would grant North Korea a maritime threat to complement his land-based nuclear firepower. "No obstacle could delay" the country's goal of bolstering its naval combat power, Kim said.

Construction of the Kang Kon ship began about a year ago at Chongjin shipyard on North Korea's east coast. That is where the vessel tipped over after a risky "side launch" — a gaffe that North Korea called an "unpardonable crime." At least four officials have been detained.

The warship remained on its side for about two weeks, covered by a blue tarp and surrounded by balloons, whose presence baffled observers. Dozens of them floated nearby, with naval experts saying they could have helped keep the vessel afloat, obscured the view from the skies or lifted objects off the destroyer. The ship's balance was restored last week and it was moved north to the Rajin port on Saturday.

The quick turnaround may mean the damage done to the ship was fairly minimal or perhaps that North Korea possesses more advanced technical know-how than previously thought, said Hong Min, a senior research fellow at the Korea Institute for National Unification, a government-funded think tank.

"We shouldn't simply dismiss their capabilities," Hong said. He doubts the North Korean warship is close to being deployable.

The North Korean warship will need considerable time to go through tests and trials at sea for deployment, according to a Thursday report by the Center for Strategic and International Studies.

Satellite imagery analyzed by CSIS shows the Kang Kon ship ready to leave the dock. But the weapons system, which was previously uncovered, remains concealed, making it difficult to know if everything is fully installed or not, according to the report.

The ship is North Korea's second modern warship, following the successful launch of its first in April. North Korea has yet to show clear video footage of the new destroyers in motion, leaving open the possibility they are tugged around by other boats, said Park Won-gon, a professor of North Korean studies at Ewha Womans University in Seoul.

"For now, it's questionable whether North Korea's warships can conduct operations at sea," Park said.

Write to Dasl Yoon at dasl.yoon@wsj.com

data.title: Shippers rise amid Iran conflict, Suez Canal risks and China-US recovery
data.description:
** Shares in shipping groups rise in early trade as analysts flag upside risks to freight rates from Red Sea disruptions, Iran tensions and likely prolonged shift away from Suez Canal

** ABG Sundal Collier analysts say Israel's strikes on Iran will delay Red Sea reopening, boosting container and car carrier demand, while reduced Iranian crude and LPG exports shift to compliant fleets, pushing tanker rates higher

** Arctic Securities analyst Kristoffer Barth Skeie adds that rising geopolitical risks in the region are expected to disrupt trade flows, making a reversal through Suez Canal less likely

** Additionally, Jefferies notes that China-US trade truce is expected to restore "regular way" volumes, paving way for significant rise in freight rates

** It adds that stable freight rates and signs of recovering China-US demand could support the sector, with limited near-term capacity and ongoing Red Sea route avoidance maintaining tight supply and pricing power

** Shares in container giants Danish Maersk and German Hapag-Lloyd are up 4.2% and 1.6%, respectively

** Tankers Frontline , Torm and Euronav rise 5.8%, 5.1% and 2.5%, respectively

data.title: Israel-Iran Escalation Could Push Oil Even Higher, Adding Inflationary Pressures — Market Talk
data.description: Brent crude prices could surge to $80-$100 a barrel if Israeli strikes were to target Iran's oil production and export facilities, potentially driving inflation higher, according to Capital Economics. Such a scenario could add about 0.5 to 1.0 percentage points to inflation in developed markets by the end of this year, economists at the firm say. "We suspect such a spike in prices would result in more OPEC+ production coming online though, thereby limiting the length of the inflation shock," they say. "But any rise in energy inflation would be another reason for central banks to proceed cautiously with cutting interest rates, and for the Fed to remain on the sidelines for now." (giulia.petroni@wsj.com)

data.title: U.S. Natural Gas Spot Prices Fall as International Prices Increase in Week Ended June 11, EIA Reports
data.description:
U.S. natural gas spot prices fell at most locations in the week ended June 11, with the benchmark Henry Hub spot price down US$0.08 to US$2.74/million British thermal units, the U.S. Energy Information Administration said in its Natural Gas Weekly Update released on Thursday.

Liquefied natural gas prices in East Asia rose US$0.14 to US$12.43/MMBtu while Title Transfer Facility natural gas prices in Europe gained US$0.24 to US$11.99/MMBtu, the EIA said, citing data from Bloomberg Finance.

The U.S. natural gas supply increased 0.5% on higher net imports from Canada, the agency reported, citing data from S&P Global Commodity Insights.

U.S. natural gas consumption rose 3.2%, Commodity Insights data showed, as power generation use jumped amid warmer weather but residential and commercial sector use declined.

Net additions to natural gas storage amounted to 109 billion cubic feet in the week ended June 6, resulting in total working gas stocks of 2,707 Bcf, the EIA said. This was 139 Bcf more than the five-year average and 256 Bcf lower than the year-ago period. The injection also marks the seventh in a row that is above 100 Bcf, the longest streak since 2014, the agency noted.

data.title: Euro Credit Default Protection Costs Rise After Israel's Strike on Iran — Market Talk
data.description: The cost of insuring euro-denominated credit against default using credit default swaps rise after Israel's attack on Iran's nuclear facilities causes investors to reduce their exposure to risky assets. "The Middle East and geopolitics more broadly is a source of risk which we cannot ignore," says Mark Dowding at RBC BlueBay Asset Management in a note. The iTraxx Europe Crossover index, which tracks euro junk bond credit default swaps, rises seven basis points to an 11-day high of 299 basis points. The iTraxx Europe Main index, which tracks euro investment-grade CDS, rises 2 basis points to 62bps. (jessica.fleetham@wsj.com)

Japan's government issued rare warnings on rising government bond yields and the changing structure of debt ownership in its economic policy roadmap as the central bank gradually trims its presence in the market.

"We must continue efforts to further promote domestic ownership of government bonds to avoid spikes in long-term interest rates caused by supply-demand imbalances," the government said in this year's economic and fiscal policy guidelines approved on Friday.

The explicit warning in the guidelines, which form the basis for budget planning, follows a recent bond market rout that briefly pushed yields on super-long Japanese government bonds (JGB) to record highs.

The government debt market, particularly the longest-dated bonds, faces a triple whammy of the Bank of Japan's tapering of bond purchases, waning demand from life insurers previously driven by capital requirements and intensifying concerns over Japan's tattered finances.

The BOJ, which owns 46% of JGBs, has been slowing bond purchases as it exits a huge asset-buying scheme.

The central bank is expected to make no big changes to the current bond taper plan at its policy meeting next week but consider slowing the pace of tapering from next fiscal year, signaling a preference to avoid big market disruptions, sources told Reuters.

That raises the importance of domestic private-sector banks, previously the biggest JGB owners, though capital rules are likely to limit their exposure to rate risks.

Foreign investors have boosted their presence in the market over the last decade, but their holding duration is typically less than those of life insurers, Koichi Sugisaki, macro strategist Morgan Stanley MUFG Securities, said.

"A situation where these buy-and-sell investors are holding large interest rate risks is inherently unstable, if not outright dangerous," Sugisaki said. "It's like having 'magma' ready to erupt at any moment, should something trigger it."

Banks owned 14.5% of JGBs including treasury discount bills as of the end of last year, while insurance firms held 15.6%. Foreign ownership stood at 11.9%.

Eager to boost purchases by stable domestic holders such as banks, the government is preparing to introduce new floating-rate bonds linked to short-term interest rates to mitigate risks from rising bond yields.

It is also considering expanding the scope of investors eligible to buy government bonds specifically designed for retail investors, allowing non-profit corporations and unlisted companies to buy such principal-guaranteed JGBs.

Growing calls from lawmakers for more stimulus and tax breaks are adding to the bond market woes.

The ruling coalition has so far resisted proposals from some opposition parties for tax breaks aimed at helping households cope with persistent inflation. It instead plans to pledge cash handouts in its campaign for an upper house election in July, which would create less fiscal strain.

In the annual policy guidelines, the government effectively pushed back the self-imposed deadline for delivering a primary budget surplus from the previous target of fiscal 2025 to "as early as possible during fiscal years 2025 to 2026."

($1 = 143.9800 yen)

data.title: Philippines' Duterte seeks interim ICC release to undisclosed country
data.description:
Key points:
  • Duterte's request cites age and humanitarian reasons
  • Counsel claims Duterte not a flight risk, lacks former influence
  • Drug war victims oppose Duterte's release

Former Philippine President Rodrigo Duterte has petitioned the International Criminal Court to allow his interim release to another country, his lawyer said in a filing, citing his advanced age and a vow not to flee or commit any crimes.

Duterte was arrested and taken to The Hague in March on murder charges linked to his "war on drugs", where thousands of alleged narcotics peddlers and users were killed. He has maintained his arrest was unlawful and tantamount to kidnapping.

Duterte's counsel Nicholas Kaufman told the ICC's pre-trial chamber a third country had expressed its "advance and principled agreement to receive Mr. Duterte onto its territory", according to the request released on Thursday. The name of the country was redacted in the text released to the public.

Duterte is not a flight risk and will not commit crimes if released, his counsel said.

"Mr Duterte is no longer the President of the Philippines, and does not command the same influence or power he is said to have abused during the period of the alleged crimes," the request stated.

Duterte was swept to power in 2016 on a signature campaign to tackle crime and eradicate illegal drugs in the Philippines. During his six years in office, 6,200 suspects were killed during anti-drug operations, according to government data, but rights groups say the actual toll was far greater.

Duterte cited humanitarian reasons in his request, saying that he is already 80 years old.

It also said the prosecution would not oppose Duterte's interim release as long as certain undisclosed terms are met.

The office of the prosecutor in an email said it would file before the ICC pre-trial chamber a response to Duterte's request, a public version of which will be available "which will clearly set out its position".

ICC assistant to counsel Kristina Conti, who represents drug war victims, told broadcaster DZMM that her clients have opposed Duterte's interim release from the start.

Despite his detention, he was elected mayor by a huge margin in his home city of Davao during May's midterm elections.

data.title: Philip Morris International Declares Regular Quarterly Dividend of $1.35 Per Share
data.description:
STAMFORD, CT--(BUSINESS WIRE)--June 13, 2025--

Regulatory News:

The Board of Directors of Philip Morris International Inc. (PMI) today declared a regular quarterly dividend of $1.35 per common share, payable on July 15, 2025, to shareholders of record as of June 27, 2025. The ex-dividend date is June 27, 2025. For more details on stock, dividends and other information, see www.pmi.com/dividend.

Philip Morris International: A Global Smoke-Free Champion

Philip Morris International is a leading international consumer goods company, actively delivering a smoke-free future and evolving its portfolio for the long term to include products outside of the tobacco and nicotine sector. The company's current product portfolio primarily consists of cigarettes and smoke-free products, including heat-not-burn, nicotine pouch and e-vapor products. As of December 31, 2024, PMI's smoke-free products were available for sale in 95 markets, and PMI estimates they were used by 38.6 million adults around the world. The smoke-free business accounted for 42% of PMI's first-quarter 2025 total net revenues. Since 2008, PMI has invested over $14 billion to develop, scientifically substantiate and commercialize innovative smoke-free products for adults who would otherwise continue to smoke, with the goal of completely ending the sale of cigarettes. This includes the building of world-class scientific assessment capabilities, notably in the areas of pre-clinical systems toxicology, clinical and behavioral research, as well as post-market studies. Following a robust science-based review, the U.S. Food and Drug Administration has authorized the marketing of Swedish Match's General snus and ZYN nicotine pouches and versions of PMI's IQOS devices and consumables - the first-ever such authorizations in their respective categories. Versions of IQOS devices and consumables and General snus also obtained the first-ever Modified Risk Tobacco Product authorizations from the FDA. With a strong foundation and significant expertise in life sciences, PMI has a long-term ambition to expand into wellness and healthcare areas and aims to enhance life through the delivery of seamless health experiences. References to "PMI", "we", "our" and "us" mean Philip Morris International Inc., and its subsidiaries. For more information, please visit www.pmi.com and www.pmiscience.com.

data.title: Oil Surge Reflects Risk Premium, Not Shifting Market Fundamentals — Market Talk
data.description: The overnight spike in oil prices reflects a significant increase in the geopolitical risk premium rather than a change in market fundamentals, according to senior economists at Capital Economics. "There does not appear to have been any direct impact on oil production or export facilities in Iran," they say. However, "ongoing uncertainty and/or an escalation in hostilities could keep the risk premium in oil prices elevated or even see it rise further." Traders are particularly concerned that Iran could retaliate by targeting the Strait of Hormuz, where around 25% of seaborne global oil supplies and 20% of global LNG transit through. However, the analysts point out that Gulf economies, which have expressed varying degrees of support for Iran, are unlikely to see direct disruptions to their own oil production facilities. (giulia.petroni@wsj.com)

data.title: India Inflation Rate Shrinks in May
data.description:
India's official rate struck a 75-month low in May, perhaps opening a pathway to additional easing from the nation's central bank.

India's headline consumer price index (CPI) in May rose 2.82% on year, cooling from the 3.16% on-tear rate logged in April, reported the National Statistics Office (NSO) on Thursday.

May's CPI result marked the fourth consecutive month that inflation has remained below the midpoint of the Reserve Bank of India (RBI) target inflation band of 4%, plus or minus 2%.

Easing food inflation helped damp the headline CPI.

The India consumer food price index (CFPI) for May rose a relatively modest 0.99% on year, after logging double-digit increases as recently as October of last year, reported the NSO.

Vegetable prices in May fell 13.7% on year, as crop shortages eased.

Housing costs in India in May rose 3.16% on year, while transportation & communication bills rose 3.85%, reported officials. Fuel & light bills rose 2.78% in the 12-month month period, added the NSO.

Encouraged by easing inflation, the RBI has been easing policy interest rates in 2025. The central bank started the year with its key policy rate at 6.50%, but has cut the rate three times, reaching 5.50% on June 6.

The RBI in June reiterated its forecast for a 6.5% expansion of India's gross domestic product (GDP) in the fiscal year started April 1, and that inflation would log under 4%.

data.title: Singapore Shares Slump Mirroring Global Losses as World Reacts to Israel Strike on Iran
data.description:
Singapore shares tanked on Friday, tracking regional and global losses as markets were weighed down by Israel's attack on Iran earlier in the day.

The Straits Times Index (STI), a key benchmark for the Singapore Exchange, ranged between 3,898.46 and 3,912.71 throughout the day. It ended the session at 3,911.42, down 10.78 points or 0.27% compared to Thursday's close.

In company news, shares of Boustead Singapore were up over 6% at the close with the company considering selling its logistics and industrial real estate assets to a real estate investment trust to be listed on the Singapore Exchange.

Union Steel Holdings was down over 3% after it reduced its stake in Eneco Energy to 24% by selling 85 million shares to investors, Eric Low and Tank Hock Guan, for about SG$978,000.

Meanwhile, Boustead Singapore said it is considering selling its logistics and industrial real estate assets to a real estate investment trust to be listed on the Singapore Exchange. Its shares closed 3% lower Friday.

data.title: Market Chatter: Apple's iPhone Sales in May Rise to Top Spot in China
data.description:
Apple 's iPhone sales in May captured the top spot in China, while global sales increased 15% on a yearly basis during April and May, Reuters reported Friday citing data from Counterpoint Research.

The tech giant also recorded double-digit growth in Japan, India and the Middle East, marking its strongest performance for the two-month period since the coronavirus pandemic, the report said.

The return to growth in Apple's two largest markets, China and the US, was seen as the primary driver of an increase in global sales, according to Counterpoint Research preliminary data.

Apple did not immediately respond to MT Newswires' request for comment on the matter.

data.title: Market Chatter: Mitsubishi to Invest in LNG Project in Canada
data.description:
Mitsubishi will invest in a 15% stake at a liquefied natural gas (LNG) project in British Columbia, Canada, Nikkei Asia reported Friday, citing a source.

This makes the conglomerate the first large-scale Japanese importing company of Canadian LNG, according to the report.

The carmaker is allotting $2.1 billion in the $14 billion LNG Canada project, equivalent to 2.1 million metric tons of the commodity out of the estimated annual production of 14 million tons, the report said.

The imports will start in July, while full commercial operation will commence by the end of the year, the report cited the source as saying.

Other investors in the project include Shell, Malaysia-based Petronas, PetroChina , and Korea Gas , Nikkei wrote.

The move comes as Japan looks for more diverse LNG sources, according to the report.
